name: Alcalá de Guadaira Green Gate Trail
name_original: Camino junto al Río Guadaira — circular desde Alcalá de Guadaíra
description: Hike the moderate 8.7-mile Alcalá de Guadaira Green Gate Trail, gaining 257 feet in 3h 36m through riverside parks and olive groves.
region_intro: Hiking around Alcalá De Guadaíra is characterized by its riverine landscapes and historical features. The region is defined by the Guadaíra River, which flows through extensive green spaces and natural monuments. Trails often follow the riverbanks, passing through parks and offering views of the Alcalá de Guadaíra Castle. The terrain typically involves gentle elevation changes along the river, with some routes incorporating historical mill sites. Best hiking trails around Alcalá De Guadaíra The most popular hiking route is Alcalá de Guadaira Green Gate Trail, an 8.6 miles (13.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 36 minutes to complete. This route follows the Guadaíra River through green spaces and historical sites. Another top favourite among local hikers is Path by Molino de las Aceñas – Municipal Swimming Pool loop from Alcalá de Guadaíra, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.5 km) path. This trail winds along the riverbanks, passing historic mills and natural areas. Local hikers also love the Realaje Mill – Vadalejos Bajo Mill loop from Alcalá de Guadaíra, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through riverside landscapes and past historical mills, often completed in about 2 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around Alcalá De Guadaíra is defined by the Guadaíra River, riverside parks, and historical mills. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Alcalá De Guadaíra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Alcalá De Guadaíra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: Alcalá De Guadaíra offers a diverse network of over 40 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These routes explore the region's unique riverine landscapes and historical sites.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: The best time to hike in Alcalá De Guadaíra is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is mild and pleasant, making it ideal for exploring the riverside parks and historical trails without the intense heat of summer.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or easy walks in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: Yes, Alcalá De Guadaíra has 17 easy hiking routes, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed stroll. These trails often follow the gentle paths along the Guadaíra River, offering accessible options for all.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: Hiking in Alcalá De Guadaíra primarily features gentle elevation changes, especially along the riverbanks. The terrain is characterized by riverside paths, green spaces, and some routes that incorporate historical mill sites. While most routes are moderate, there are also some more challenging options with slightly greater elevation gains.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest to see along the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Alcalá De Guadaíra lead you past significant historical landmarks. You can explore the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/385597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de Guadaíra Castle&lt;/a&gt;, perched above the river, or discover the series of historic water mills, such as the Molino de las Aceñas, along the 'Route of the Mills'. The distinctive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7000285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Dragón&lt;/a&gt; (Dragon Bridge) is also a unique architectural highlight.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Alcalá De Guadaíra are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21828990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path by Molino de las Aceñas – Municipal Swimming Pool loop from Alcalá de Guadaíra&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 9.5 km loop along the riverbanks.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Guadaíra River?
A: The Guadaíra River is central to the hiking experience in Alcalá De Guadaíra. Many trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010622589/alcala-de-guadaira-green-gate-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de Guadaira Green Gate Trail&lt;/a&gt;, closely follow the riverbanks, winding through the extensive green spaces and natural monuments that define the region.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Alcalá De Guadaíra suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, the region offers many family-friendly options, especially the easier routes along the river. The extensive riverside parks like Parque Oromana and Parque de San Juan provide safe and enjoyable environments for children to explore while hiking.

Q: Is there public transport access to the hiking trailheads in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: Alcalá De Guadaíra is well-integrated with public transport, and many trailheads are accessible from within the town. The urban area itself is surrounded by the river and its parks, making it convenient to reach starting points for various routes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: The hiking trails in Alcalá De Guadaíra are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the beautiful riverine landscapes, the historical mills, and the well-maintained paths that offer a blend of nature and heritage.

Q: Are there any specific parks or natural areas worth exploring in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3929111/natural-monuments-around-alcala-de-guadaira" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riberas del Guadaíra Natural Monument&lt;/a&gt; is a key area, encompassing a 10 km section of the river with 120 hectares of green spaces. Within this, you'll find notable parks like Parque Oromana, known for its pine forest, and Parque del Puente del Dragón, offering scenic walks around the unique Dragon Bridge.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Alcalá De Guadaíra?
A: Many of the trails and riverside parks in Alcalá De Guadaíra are dog-friendly, making it a great destination for hiking with your canine companion. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to maintain the natural beauty of the area for everyone.
tips: Better to take this path and not follow the official marked path

A late medieval Aceña that passes next to the Guadaira River and that was the nucleus of an old Almohad town from the 13th century. The Molino de Cerrajas is the last mill of Guadaíra belonging to Alcalá de Guadaíra, before the city of Seville.
Aqueduct that goes over the river and is current, don't think you will find a Roman aqueduct but one that is a few centuries old. Still, it's nice to walk over it.

Well-preserved mill located on the other side of the river. White in color, eye-catching as it stands out from the rest of the surroundings and where it forms a beautiful postcard together with the river and the town of Alcalá de Guadaíra in the background.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty and terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The Alcalá de Guadaira Green Gate Trail is generally considered moderate, as indicated by komoot's rating. However, many sections are described as easy and accessible, suitable for various fitness levels. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces, including paved paths, rural tracks, and unpaved ground, especially outside urban areas. The elevation changes are relatively low, contributing to its overall accessibility.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse landscape. You'll enjoy views of the Guadaíra River, passing through riverside parks like Parque San Francisco and Parque Oromana. Further along, the route winds through olive groves, offering magnificent vistas of the rolling 'campiña' (countryside). The trail also highlights the natural beauty of the Riberas del Guadaíra Natural Monument, with its extensive green areas and diverse birdlife.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks or points of interest will I see?
faqs.answer: This trail is rich in history. You'll pass by several ancient flour mills, some dating back to Islamic times, such as the Cerrajas Mill and Realaje Mill. The route also includes the impressive El Viar Aqueduct and follows sections of old railway lines, famously known as the 'Tren de los Panaderos'.

faqs.question: Is the Alcalá de Guadaira Green Gate Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. Many sections are suitable for walking with dogs, especially the wider paths and natural areas. However, always keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near other hikers and cyclists, and remember to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot in this region, so if you visit then, it's best to go early in the morning or late in the afternoon.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or the Natural Monument?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Alcalá de Guadaira Green Gate Trail or the Riberas del Guadaíra Natural Monument. It is freely accessible to the public for recreational activities.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, as the trail passes through Alcalá de Guadaíra and connects to other towns, you can often find parking in or near the urban centers where the trail begins or passes through. Look for public parking options in Alcalá de Guadaíra, particularly near the river parks or the start of the Green Gate sections.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Alcalá de Guadaira Green Gate Trail is part of a larger network. It overlaps significantly with the 'Puerta Verde de Alcalá de Guadaira' and sections of the 'Vía Verde de los Alcores'. Additionally, parts of this Green Gate are also integrated into the Camino de Santiago, specifically the Vía Augusta coming from Cádiz.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for cycling?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is very popular with cyclists. Many sections, particularly those that follow old railway lines or are part of the 'Puerta Verde' network, have mostly paved surfaces and are well-suited for cycling, offering an easy and enjoyable ride.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: As the trail passes through and near Alcalá de Guadaíra and connects to other towns like Mairena del Alcor and El Viso del Alcor, you will find opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and restrooms in these urban areas. It's advisable to plan your stops, especially on longer sections between towns.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for sun exposure, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), comfortable hiking shoes suitable for both paved and unpaved surfaces, and snacks. A map or GPS device is also useful, though the trail is generally well-marked. Binoculars could enhance birdwatching opportunities.
